finding_results.evidence: Post-revision counts verified by grep: (A) 'couldn't' appears 3 times — chapters/chapter_04.md:123 ('couldn't argue with' at adaptation-circle pivot), chapters/chapter_04.md:185 ('couldn't hold in one thought' at data-vs-child pivot), chapters/chapter_04.md:199 ('couldn't find it' in final paragraph). All three fall at the chapter's emotional pivot points. (B) 'the kind of' appears 3 times — chapters/chapter_04.md:95, chapters/chapter_04.md:127, chapters/chapter_04.md:189. (C) 'the way [clause]' appears 3 times — chapters/chapter_04.md:23, chapters/chapter_04.md:87, chapters/chapter_04.md:185. (D) 'He also [verb]' appears 1 time — chapters/chapter_04.md:61.
finding_results.notes: Removed 8 'couldn't' instances (lines 11, 19, 25, 27×2, 45, 47, 75, 83, 185) using varied strategies: physical substitution (line 25: 'something in Dan's chest loosened'; line 45: 'the part that wouldn't let him rest'), trailing syntax (line 27: 'wouldn't hold still long enough to name'), mid-clause pivot (line 47: 'dissolved the moment he tried to put words on them'; line 75: 'his mind stalled on the fact'), direct rendering (line 83: 'refused, on first look, to resolve into meaning'), and sensory landing (line 185: 'landed somewhere else entirely'). Removed 3 'the kind of' instances (lines 15, 63, 81) via direct rendering or construction deletion. Removed 3 'the way' instances (lines 19, 29, 59, 103, 119, 183) via construction substitution ('as', 'just as', 'how') or sentence restructuring. Removed 2 'He also' instances (lines 27, 87) by merging with adjacent clauses. No sanitization introduced; all replacements preserve or strengthen the chapter's embodied specificity and Dan's inarticulation under pressure.

summary: Single finding (F-CH04-PROSE-02) fully addressed. Four formula families reduced to target ceilings: 'couldn't' from 10 to 3 (retained at emotional pivots only), 'the kind of' from 6 to 3, 'the way [clause]' from 6 to 3, 'He also [verb]' from 3 to 1. Replacement strategies dramatize Dan's inarticulation through physical substitution, trailing syntax, mid-clause pivots, and direct rendering rather than repeating the mechanical 'couldn't' inability construction. No new continuity contradictions introduced. Heading contract preserved. Dialogue register and character voices unchanged. No sanitization or aesthetic risk regression.
